scope.summary:
Goal: Implement multi-cursor editing and column/box selection parity with the React Monaco surface. Monaco supports: Alt+Click to add a cursor, Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down to add cursor above/below, Alt+Shift+drag for box selection, Ctrl+D to select the next occurrence of the current word. All four must work in the native editor. Expected diff shape: new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/cursor.rs` containing the cursor and selection model, and edits to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` to handle multi-cursor input and render all cursors.

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/cursor.rs` — defines `Cursor { anchor: usize, head: usize }` (byte offsets into the TextBuffer), `Selection` (alias for a `Cursor` where anchor != head), `CursorSet { cursors: Vec<Cursor> }`. Methods on `CursorSet`: `add_cursor(byte_offset: usize)` (deduplicates by offset), `remove_overlap()` (merges cursors whose selections overlap), `move_all(direction: MoveDir, buffer: &TextBuffer)` (moves all cursors), `insert_at_all(text: &str, buffer: &mut TextBuffer)` (inserts text at each cursor, adjusting subsequent cursor offsets), `delete_at_all(buffer: &mut TextBuffer)` (deletes the character/selection at each cursor). `MoveDir` enum: `Left, Right, Up, Down, LineStart, LineEnd, WordLeft, WordRight`.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `cursor_set: CursorSet` to `CodeEditorPanel`. Process egui input events: `PointerButton::Primary` click sets single cursor; `Alt` + click adds a cursor; `Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down` adds cursor above/below; `Alt+Shift` drag sets box selection (compute column range from drag start/end, add one cursor per line in range); `Ctrl+D` finds next occurrence of the word at the primary cursor and adds a selection cursor on it.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/panel.rs` — render all cursors as 2px vertical bars using `ui.painter().rect_filled()` at the correct pixel position (use `y_for_line` from MT-002 and character-width from the monospace font metrics). Render selections as colored rect overlays (semi-transparent). For column/box selections, render one rectangle per line in the column range.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `egui::PointerState::button_clicked`, `egui::InputState::modifiers` — for detecting Alt+click and Ctrl+D.
- `egui::Painter::rect_filled` — draw cursor caret and selection highlight.
- `egui::FontId` and `ui.fonts().glyph_width()` — measure monospace character width for column alignment.
- No additional crates needed.
- AccessKit: add `author_id = "code_editor_cursor_{n}"` `Role::TextCursor` for each cursor (n = cursor index) via `accesskit::NodeBuilder`. Reuse the existing AccessKit integration in `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/` (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs) for node registration and tree snapshotting — do not stand up a new AccessKit pipeline.

React reference (Monaco parity surface):
- Monaco multi-cursor: Alt+Click, Ctrl+Alt+Up, Ctrl+Alt+Down, Alt+Shift drag, Ctrl+D — these are the canonical VS Code multi-cursor keybindings. The native editor must respond to the same bindings.
- `app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx` — Monaco handles multi-cursor internally; here the native implementation must replicate the behavior.
- `app/src/lib/editor/editor_keymap.ts` — the keymap system used by the rich editor; the code editor has its own keymap (see MT-010) but Ctrl+D and Alt+Click must be wired now as they are core to multi-cursor.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Implement `CursorSet` in `cursor.rs`. Keep cursors sorted by `head` byte offset at all times (sort after each mutation). `remove_overlap` merges any two cursors where one's selection range contains the other's head.
2. In `CodeEditorPanel::show()`, after the ScrollArea, check `ui.input(|i| i.events.clone())` for: `egui::Event::PointerButton { button: Primary, pressed: true, modifiers: { alt: true, .. } }` -> add cursor at clicked position; `egui::Event::Key { key: D, modifiers: { ctrl: true }, pressed: true }` -> Ctrl+D logic (see step 4).
3. For Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down: detect `egui::Event::Key { key: Up/Down, modifiers: { ctrl: true, alt: true }, pressed: true }` -> add cursor one line above/below each existing cursor.
4. Ctrl+D implementation: get the word at the primary cursor's head byte offset using `buffer.word_at(byte_offset)` (implement this helper: scan left/right from offset while char is alphanumeric or `_`). Search the buffer bytes for the next occurrence after the primary cursor. Add a new cursor with anchor=occurrence_start, head=occurrence_end.
5. Box/column selection: track `drag_start: Option<(line, col)>` in CodeEditorPanel state. On Alt+Shift drag, compute the column range as `(min_col..=max_col)` and the line range as `(min_line..=max_line)`. Replace cursor_set with one cursor per line in the range, each selecting from column start to column end on that line.
6. Render: for each cursor in cursor_set, compute pixel rect (x from column * glyph_width, y from `y_for_line(line)`, w=2, h=line_height for caret; x from selection_start_col * glyph_width, w = selection_width_chars * glyph_width for selection rect). Use `egui::Color32::from_rgba_unmultiplied(100, 149, 237, 80)` for selection overlay.
7. On text input (key events that produce chars), call `cursor_set.insert_at_all(text, &mut buffer)` — must adjust each subsequent cursor's byte offset by the byte length of the inserted text.

Reuse pointers (real WP-011 modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/):
- Shared selection/clipboard/command bus: reuse `command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs` for dispatching the multi-cursor commands (Alt+Click add-cursor, Ctrl+D select-next-occurrence, Ctrl+Alt+Up/Down add-cursor-above/below) and for broadcasting selection-change events; do not invent a new interaction bus.
- Command palette / Ctrl+D wiring: register the multi-cursor commands through `command_palette.rs` + `command_registry.rs` so they are discoverable and rebindable.
- AccessKit cursor nodes: reuse `accessibility/` (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs).

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 DONE (TextBuffer exists with byte-offset API).
Risk if missed: Multi-cursor is a core VS Code parity feature; operators who use column-select for bulk edits will be blocked. MT-004 (find/replace) and MT-010 (keymap) both build on the cursor model.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native cursor` passes — tests: add two cursors, verify both present; add overlapping cursors, verify merged; insert text with two cursors, verify both insertions happened and offsets are correct after the second insertion.
AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native cursor_column_select` passes — test: column select lines 2-4 columns 3-7 on a 5-line buffer, verify 3 cursors each with correct anchor/head byte offsets.

AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native ctrl_d` passes — test: buffer 'foo\nfoo\nbar', cursor at line 1 col 1, Ctrl+D adds selection at line 2 col 0-3 (second 'foo' occurrence), second Ctrl+D wraps to line 1.
AC-004: egui_kittest interactive test simulates Alt+Click at two positions and verifies two cursor nodes in the AccessKit tree (author_id='code_editor_cursor_0' and 'code_editor_cursor_1').
AC-005: egui_kittest screenshot shows two distinct cursor carets (two vertical pixel columns of cursor color) for a two-cursor state.
AC-006: `insert_at_all` with two cursors at offsets 0 and 5 inserting 'X' results in buffer text with 'X' at position 0 and 'X' at position 6 (offset adjusted by 1 for the first insertion).
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native cursor` — GREEN, logged.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native cursor_column_select` — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native ctrl_d` — GREEN, logged.
PT-004: egui_kittest test `multi_cursor_accesskit` (src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_multi_cursor_accesskit.rs) — AccessKit JSON dump contains both cursor_0 and cursor_1 nodes.
PT-005: egui_kittest screenshot `test_output/MT-003-two-cursors.png` pixel-verified for two caret columns.
implementation_notes: Cursor byte offsets must always be clamped to `0..=buffer.len_bytes()` — never allow an out-of-bounds cursor.
After every CursorSet mutation, call `remove_overlap()` then re-sort by head offset.
For `insert_at_all`: process cursors in reverse order by head offset so earlier insertions don't invalidate later offsets.
Character width for column alignment: use `ui.fonts(|f| f.glyph_width(&FontId::monospace(font_size), ' '))` — all monospace chars have the same width.
Do not use `egui::TextEdit` — we own the text buffer and render it manually.

Cross-WP dependency: this MT builds on the WP-KERNEL-011 native shell. The TextBuffer (MT-001 of this WP) plus the WP-011 AccessKit pipeline in `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/` and the command bus in `command_registry.rs` + `event_bus.rs` must already exist; wire the multi-cursor commands through those existing surfaces rather than creating new ones.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/MonacoCodeBlockView.tsx — Monaco multi-cursor is built-in; this MT reimplements equivalent behavior natively. Study the escape-to-prose handler (lines 89-97) for focus management patterns.

app/src/lib/editor/editor_keymap.ts — EDITOR_KEY_BINDINGS table for Mod-f, Mod-h patterns; Ctrl+D is a Monaco built-in not in the Rich editor keymap, but must be added to the code editor keymap (MT-010 will formalize the table; wire Ctrl+D here as a direct input handler routed through command_registry.rs).
binds_backend_api: None — cursor and selection state is purely in-process.

risks: RISK-001: Offset drift bug — after inserting at cursor N, cursor N+1's byte offset is not adjusted, causing subsequent cursors to point to wrong positions. Mitigate: process cursors in reverse sorted order; unit test specifically for this case.
RISK-002: Column select with lines of different lengths — a column at col 10 on a line of length 6 must clamp to line end. Mitigate: clamp column to line length in `cursor.rs`.
RISK-003: Ctrl+D infinite loop — if there is only one occurrence, second Ctrl+D must not loop forever. Mitigate: wrap search; if start == primary cursor head after wrap, stop.
RISK-004: AccessKit cursor node flood for very large cursor sets (e.g. 1000 cursors from column select). Mitigate: cap AccessKit cursor nodes at 64; emit only the first 64 cursors in the tree.
minimum_controls: MC-001: offset-adjustment unit test is a required AC — must be in CI.
MC-004: AccessKit cap at 64 cursors documented in code comment.
